response.justification: Candidate is a strong senior IC with 13+ years of polyglot software development and clear craftsmanship values (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based, workshops). However, the role is a Software Engineering Manager focused on developer tools/build/test infrastructure for a mobile platform, and the resume shows no formal people-management experience, no documented organizational leadership of engineers, and limited direct work on compilers, build systems, or large-scale internal developer tooling at scale. Mobile-platform exposure is also thin (some iOS/Apple TV work in 2015–2016). Better suited for a Staff IC or Tech Lead role than a Level II Engineering Manager.
strengths: Deep, polyglot engineering background (13+ years) with strong testing/CI/CD and developer-tooling instincts (test runner, assertion library, MCP tooling).
Demonstrated technical leadership signals: founder of an open-source platform, workshops, courses, and conference talks on testing and web internals.
Hands-on experience with build/release-adjacent work (GitLab, CI servers, Docker, Artifactory) and AI-agent/developer tooling at re:cinq.
concerns: No explicit people-management experience (1:1s, performance reviews, hiring) — the role requires 2+ years managing people.
Limited mobile-platform depth; brief iOS/Apple TV stint in 2015–2016 is dated relative to a role supporting a major mobile ecosystem.
Developer-tools experience is adjacent (test libs, CI setup) rather than core build systems/compilers/test infrastructure at organizational scale.
